topic: Electro template placeholder routes cause false findings
context: Live Electro template routes default-named inserts 22-115 to Instruments Mix as placeholders.
observation: Outgoing non-Master routes on default-named empty tracks are template reservation evidence, not necessarily audio occupancy.
result: Suppress missing_hpf, ungrouped, audible-track, and unused-cleanup findings for these tracks after matching the Electro topology.
source_method: Read-only live dump and probes on FL Studio Producer Edition v25.2.5 [build 5055], controller build channels-v38.
example: Insert 22 -> Instruments ► Mix @0.8 should not be flagged as missing_hpf or unused_mixer_track in Electro.
known_pitfalls: Do not globally ignore all default-named routed tracks.
Match a recognizable template topology before suppressing findings.
Do not treat template reservation as proof that a track contains audio.
open_questions: Do the other 12 templates use similar placeholder route banks?
Should template reservations be exposed as info-level context in user-facing reports?
next_recommended_action: Add a topology classifier and fixture-backed tests before changing user-facing findings.
